observations.recommendation: 1.6.	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) directs the Municipal Treasurer/GSO in coordination with the committee members to; (a) initiate the conduct of physical inventory and/or verification of all properties of the municipal government; and (b) prepare and submit the corresponding RPCPPE to the audit team to establish the existence, accuracy and validity of reported balance of the PPE in the financial statements.

2.4.	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be directed to take-up the money value of the earned leave credits of the officers and employees of the municipality to have a fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

4.5.	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be directed to thoroughly review past transactions, reconcile the reciprocal accounts and effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances in order that a reliable data is presented.

5.4.	We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant be directed to (a) prepare the necessary adjusting JEVs to reclassify the accounts; and (b) to establish mechanism to ensure the transactions is recorded in an appropriate account classification.

6.8.	We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant be directed to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the details of the Loans Payable – Domestic account to provide clarity and divulge information that are not readily available on the face of the financial statements as prescribed in IPSAS No. 1.

We recommended that the LCE directs the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the rules and regulations on the preparation of the financial statements. Also, the Municipal Accountant to revisit the provisions of the IPSAS and other COA issuances particularly on the preparation and presentation of accounts in the financial statements

8.8.	We recommended management to issue final demand letter to the concerned AOs including those separated, resigned, transferred and retired from service.  Failure of the AO to settle their outstanding cash advance within the period specified in the final demand letter, we recommend that management take appropriate remedial actions provided by the laws, rules and regulations.

8.9.	We further recommended that in cases where the conditions provided for in the COA Circular No. 2016-005 are met, the LCE shall file the request for authority to write-off the dormant unliquidated cash advances to the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/or Supervising Auditor (SA) following the procedures and specific guidelines set forth in Paragraph 8.0 of the said Circular.

9.6.	We recommended that Management submit a written explanation (a) for the very low percentage of implemented projects under the 20% EDF. Also, we reiterate our previous recommendation that the LCE to prioritize the implementation of the development projects so that the expected output/outcome of the projects will be attained. Moreover, ensure that Municipal Planning and Development Office and Municipal Engineering Office regularly monitor the status of project implementation so that necessary interventions may be introduced at the earliest time.

10.7.	We recommended that management submit the documents pertaining to all suspensions and/or time extensions of the above-mentioned projects.

10.8.	Moving forward, we recommended that management: (a) require the personnel of the Municipal Engineering Office to conduct regular monitoring and supervision of projects to ensure that infrastructure projects are accomplished in accordance with the terms of the contract and document the same; and (b) impose liquidated damages and other remedies provided by the rules and regulations for any unjustified violation of the terms and conditions of the contract.

11.8.	We recommended that the LCE requires the Municipal Engineering Office to immediately submit the pertinent documents of the above projects to facilitate the conduct of the technical review and/or inspections. Also, we recommended that management institute measures to ensure strict compliance with the above-mentioned regulations.
12.7.	We recommended that the BAC institute mechanism to ensure the compliance of the procurement activities with the provisions of the RA No. 9184 and its RIRR.

13.4.	We recommended that management require: (a) the compliance of the documentary requirements prescribed in Item 3.1.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 before the funds will be release to the concerned barangay; and (b) the recipient barangays to submit the report on the utilization (liquidation report) of the funds.

14.6.	We recommended that Management comply with the requirement of the above-cited regulations for the GFPS members to be guided on the GAD policies to ensure that the programs, projects and activities of the municipality will be GAD responsive. Also, to establish mechanism to ensure that the GFPS are performing their mandated functions.

15.7.	We recommended that management comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013 -004 dated January 30, 2013 on Information and Publicity on PPA of government agencies, particularly on the posting of Signboards specified in Annex “A” and the general guidelines, and reporting and monitoring. Also, to regularly update the status of the PPAs in the signboards.

We recommended that the Management to evaluate the strategic location of the dumpsite by considering the factors surrounding the area and its long-term effect. 

Also, to stop the operation of the open dumpsite for its garbage disposal and instead to use controlled dumpsite if sanitary landfill is not warranted considering the financial capability of the LGU.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Treasurer to enforce collection of the delinquent real property taxes by conducting tax education campaign, otherwise avail of the remedies provided in Sections 256 to 260 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the management to prioritize the completion of the necessary amenities of the local government disaster recovery facility to ensure efficient and effective response in times of calamity.

We recommended that the official drivers of the Local Government and other concerned personnel to prepare and submit the Monthly Report of Official Travel and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption pursuant to COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977. 

The Monthly reports will provide a summary of trips per vehicle in chronological order based on the serially numbered Driver’s Trip Tickets.

Evaluate the staffing pattern of the local government and consider the creation of the separate GSO with staff/personnel to perform the mandated functions of the GSO; 

Require the GSO or designated personnel to submit a copy of the contracts, purchase orders and notice of delivery to the audit team within the prescribed period.

We recommended that Management to comply with the requirement of the above-cited regulations for the GFPS members to be guided on the GAD policies to ensure that the programs, projects and activities of the municipality will be GAD responsive. 

Also, to establish mechanism to ensure that the GFPS are performing their mandated functions.

We recommend that Management to coordinate with the PhilHealth Regional Offices (PROs) within their respective catchment areas for the coverage of government hired JOs and PBCs under the program. 

Also, to submit justification for non-compliance with the program of PhilHealth.

We recommended that the Management to require the OIC-Municipal Accountant in coordination with the concerned officials (Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Assessor, and Municipal Engineer) to work-back the details of the beginning balance of the above-mentioned accounts. 

Also, for the ensuring years to: 

Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor to provide the schedule of the Real Property/SE Tax Receivable every reporting period;

Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to maintain a separate subsidiary ledger per fund received from NGAs and the Municipal Engineer to prepare the project cost sheet per project; and

Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to ensure that all the accounts are supported with schedule and submit the financial reports within the prescribed period.

We recommended that management shall strictly adhere to the guidelines in the granting, utilization and accounting of financial assistance to NGOs/POs as embodied in COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007 to ensure transparency and accountability of government transactions.

We recommended that the Management to allocate one percent (1%) of its Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for the implementation of the LCPC PPAs in line with the adopted child delinquency prevention plan and Annual Work and Financial Plan of the Council. 

Also, to ensure that the PPAs to be funded out of the fund are in compliance with the above-mentioned DILG Memorandum Circular in order to maximize the capability of the Municipality in addressing the issues and protecting the interest of the children in the locality.
We recommended that management adhere to the reporting requirements by preparing the required financial statement as prescribed above.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to:
Ensure that the projects will be utilized in accordance with the intended purpose to avoid its immediate deterioration and wastage of government resources.

We recommended that management refrain from authorizing deductions from the salaries of the employees that will reduce their net take home pay below P5,000.00 in 
CY 2018. Deductions from salaries should be prioritized as detailed in the GAA.

We recommended that the management to strictly follow the guidelines on the Local Roads Asset Management System provided in the COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015 in accounting and reporting all its local roads and road network system.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management to meticulously and judiciously plan the procurement process and the procurement of goods to be made through competitive bidding pursuant to Sections 7 and 10 of the revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and to refrain from paying goods on reimbursement basis without following the procurement process provided in RA 9184 and its IRR.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Assessor to prioritize the titling of the municipal lots for its protection from third party claims.
We recommended that Management initiate measures to collect the delinquent accounts from market rentals and waterworks system amounting to P668, 270.00.

We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer prepare the internal guidelines for approval of the Local Chief Executive of the specific criteria, administrative procedures and standards on the grant of Magna Carta benefits to all entitled PHWs and use the new rate of the medical personnel in computing the hazard pay.

Require the Accountant to submit the Monthly and Quarterly Trial Balances and Disbursement Vouchers with the supporting documents within the prescribed period as required by existing regulations to avoid sanctions for non-compliance of the requirement and the possible institution of disciplinary action against the erring employee.

We recommended that all collections should be deposited intact daily to the Liquidating Officer and/or authorized depository bank strictly in accordance with the provision cited under Section 32 of COA Circular 2002-003 and Joint Circular 1-81 of DOF and COA.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive designates any  employee who is holding a regular plantilla position to act as the General Services Officer under the direct supervision of the Municipal Treasurer who will take charged on the conduct of physical inventory of PPE items; creates Inventory committee with the composition stated above to facilitate the physical count and/or verification of all PPE of the Municipality; and prepare and submit the result of the factual verification through RPCPPE to the audit team.

We recommend the following:
Strictly enforce the liquidation of all cash advances within thirty (30) days upon return to the permanent official station;
Demand letter shall be made by the Municipal Accountant for the immediate liquidation and settlement of all cash advances outstanding as of December 31, 2020, from concerned officials and employees, including the suspension of salaries; and
Strictly adhere to applicable rules and regulations in the grant, utilization, and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) directs the concerned Officers and/or employees to deposit the amount with the Authorized Government Depository bank immediately.
Moving forward, we recommended that the LCE requires the accountable officer to strictly observe the rules and regulations pertaining to the collections and deposits of the government funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer strengthen cash transactions' efficiency and effectiveness by implementing effective internal control. Furthermore, the Municipal Treasure must observe the following:
1.	There must be a clear-cut separation of the cashiering function from the accounting function.
2. 	The Municipal Treasurer/Liquidating Officer must record the collections and deposits daily in the cash book.
3. 	Collections must be deposited intact and as frequently as required by regulations.
4. 	The cashbook balance must be reconciled with the cash on hand at the close of each day.
5. 	Cashbook balances must be reconciled with the subsidiary ledgers at least once every quarter.
6. 	Monthly reports of accountability for accountable forms must be prepared and submitted.
7. 	The Accountable Form Custodian must adequately control the ordering, storing, issuing, and recording of unused official receipts and other accountable forms.
8. 	The person who made a deposit must be escorted by armed guards and provided with transportation equipment.
9. 	A responsible officer of the agency must closely supervise the collecting officers.
10. 	The stock of unused checks must be accessible only to custodians and those who prepare and sign checks.
11. 	Voided or spoiled checks must be marked cancelled and held available for subsequent inspection.
12. 	Cash advances of the Disbursing Officer must not exceed the actual amount to be disbursed.

We recommended that the LCE directs the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer to reconcile their records in order to come up with more reliable costing of the PPE accounts; and moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant be directed to draw journal entry voucher to adjust/correct the affected accounts if necessary.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provides detailed information regarding the CIP accounts to have a fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements and to prepare and maintain CIP Ledger Cards (CIPLCs) on a per project basis and to effect the transfer of completed projects, if any, from the CIP accounts to the appropriate PPE accounts and ensure that proper accounts are used in recording transactions.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant be directed to (a) prepare the necessary adjusting JEVs to reclassify the accounts; and (b) to establish mechanism to ensure the transactions is recorded in an appropriate account classification.

We recommended that the Accountant make a detailed review of the accounts and determine the cause of the unreconciled amounts and make the necessary corrections in the books of accounts to present the corrected balances of the affected accounts. It is also recommended that the Municipal Accountant be directed to regularly reconcile the balances of reciprocal accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain a separate special account in the General Fund for loans wherein the receipts and expenditures thereof are recorded so that accurate and separate information on the status of loans are readily available for decision making purposes; and observed timely payments of its loan amortization and interests due to avoid incurrence of penalties on the failure of such.
It is also recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant be directed to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the details of the Loans Payable – Domestic account to provide clarity and divulge information that are not readily available on the face of the financial statements as prescribed.

We recommended that the LCE directs the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the rules and regulations on the preparation of the financial statements. Also, the Municipal Accountant to revisit the provisions of the IPSAS and other COA issuances particularly on the preparation and presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the BAC or BAC Secretariat submit the contracts together with the documentary requirements enumerated in the checklists for submission to the COA Audit Team for the auditorial and legal review and ultimately to the COA Technical Audit Services depending on the amount involved.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepares the Report on Local Road Network and Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System with the Inventory Committee's help. Furthermore, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain the subsidiary ledger for roads and their components, reconcile it with the result of the physical count at year-end, correctly depreciate the accounts, and disclose it in the Notes to the Financial Statements as prescribed.

We recommended that (1) the Municipality submit an explanation or justification as to the reason why RED CAT INTERNATIONAL LTD. was named the beneficiary in the LC instead of AVANTRAC Heavy Machinery, Inc., the supplier in whose name the contract was awarded; and, (2) the Municipality refrain from facilitating the availability of a letter of credit to a foreign entity, who is not a winning bidder and not a party to the contract by and between the Municipality of Alicia, under section 43.1.1 of RA 9184 and NPM 051-2004, dated April 16, 2004, of the Non-Policy Opinions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor directs the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to submit all the required monthly financial reports necessary in the audit on or before the due date.

We recommended that the management discontinue accepting collection receipts in place of the sales invoice or official receipts as proof of payment. On the other hand, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer demand from the supplier for the sales invoices or official receipts to obtain principal evidence on the payment of goods.

We recommended that the Municipality comply with the requirements prescribed under Local Budget Circular (LBC) 125 and submit the monthly report using the prescribed format (Annex C) to the auditor's office.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive be compliant with the general guidelines, reporting and monitoring the information and publicity on Programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs) as imposed in COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 31, 2013. In line with this, the management shall (a) Submit to the Audit Team the list of all on-going government Programs/Projects/Activities (PPAs) and those that are to be implemented during the year, (b) Inform this Office within ten (10) days after the infrastructure project's award or before starting the program/ activity that the appropriate project signboards and public notices are already posted, (c) and submit to this Office the monthly monitoring report on PPAs.

We recommended that management to strictly comply with relevant provisions of laws and rules governing the administration of LDRRMF for government resources to be assured of having been administered and appropriately used, regularly, and legally according to the purposes for which the fund was appropriated.

We recommended that management direct the timely submission of the Monthly Report on the Utilization of the LDRRMF by the MDRRMO- designate as certified correct by the Municipal Accountant to the Office of the Auditor as required under item 25, Section 4, Rule 6 of IRR of RA 10121 and detailed under item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommend that management submit written justification on the procurement of medical supplies with prices exceeding the price ceiling provided by the Department of Health. Otherwise, require the supplier to refund the excess amount computed based on the DOH Memorandum Nos. 2020-0144A and 2020-0250A.

We further recommend that management observe the Drug Price Reference Index and other related issuances of the Department of Health in the succeeding procurement of medicines and medical supplies pursuant to Republic Act No. 7581 and DOH Administrative Order No. 2014-0001.

We recommend that the management submit the lacking supporting documents noted above to account for the procured food supplies and other relief goods.

Further, strictly observe and comply with established laws, rules and regulations on documentary requirements in the disbursement of public funds, pursuant to Section 4 of Presidential Decree No. 1445, among other pertinent provisions, to safeguard government funds and property.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant follow the Perpetual Inventory Method in accounting for supplies in order to establish control in the receipt and disposition of the inventory items.

We also recommend that the Property Officer prepare and submit Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) and the monthly Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) to the accounting office as basis in preparing a journal entry to record the issuances/expenses.

We further recommend that a physical count of all undistributed/ unconsumed items be conducted to determine the cost of the items that will be restored back to the inventory account.

We recommend that the Property Officer initiate the preparation of stock/property cards for all property, plant and equipment of the municipality.

We further recommend that management require the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of the property, plant and equipment and submit copies of the reports to the Municipal Accountant for the proper recording of adjustments and to the Auditor for verification.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers to show details for each control account in the general ledger, particularly the accounts with no or incomplete records, pursuant to Sections 4(g) and Sections 4(i) of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs.

We further recommend that balances per subsidiary ledgers be reconciled with the corresponding GL controlling account at the end of each month.

We recommend that management requires the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to immediately effect the actual transfer of funds for the intra-agency receivables and payables of the municipality to balance out the cash back up per fund.

We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer made regular replenishment or transfers from the SEF, Trust Fund and Special Accounts to the General Fund Proper for funds used to remit taxes and other liabilities.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant issue demand letters to the recipient of fund transfers for the submission of liquidations reports or settlement of receivables.

We further recommend that management discontinue the practice of granting fund transfers to the Implementing Agencies with unliquidated balances and a certification by the Accountant be required for succeeding transfers.

We recommend that management submit the written communication duly received by the contractor on the follow-up/demand to fast track the project implementation.

We further recommend that management ensure that liquidated damages for the delayed implementation be duly deducted on the claims of the contractor.

We recommend that the Disbursing Officer prepare the Report of Cash Disbursements to liquidate his/her cash advances for payment of salaries, wages, honoraria, allowances, and other personnel benefits, current operating expenses, and special purpose/time-bound undertakings.

We also recommend that the MSWDO, with the assistance of the Barangay Senior Citizens Association, update and make corrections on the list of indigent senior citizens and in the certificate of eligibility to reflect the accurate details of the beneficiaries.

We further recommend that management make written representations to the DSWD for the release of the unclaimed Social Pension after locating, identifying and giving due notice to the representatives of the CY 2020 deceased beneficiaries and COVID19-stranded senior citizens.

We recommend that management submit the Fuel Consumption Report for the disbursements enumerated to determine the validity in utilizing the Bayanihan Grant to Cities and Municipalities (BGCM) Fund for the fuel, oil and lubricants totaling ₱494,092.00.

We further recommend that management enforce the use of a properly accomplished Driver’s Trip Ticket, Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption to control the use of the motor vehicles and to account for the fuel, oil and lubricants consumption of the municipality. Otherwise, no disbursement voucher for fuel consumption (gasoline and oil) of government vehicles shall be allowed in audit unless duly supported by properly accomplished and approved serially numbered driver’s trip tickets.
We recommend that the practice of pre-signing checks be discontinued immediately.

We recommend that management discontinue the practice of granting cash advances to the elected officials for purposes other than that stated in Item 4.1.4 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997; And succeeding cash advances for field/activity current operating expenses be granted to permanently appointed employee duly designated as disbursing officer.

We further recommend that management ensure that a certification from the Accountant that previous cash advance have been liquidated and accounted for in the books is required to support the grant of new/additional cash advances.

We recommend that management assign personnel who will prepare the report and submit as soon as possible its AAPSI for the AAR 2019.

Further, we recommend that management comply with the provision and submit the action plan within sixty (60) days upon receipt of the Annual Audit Report in its ensuing years.

We recommend that management make written representation to the Bureau of Internal Revenue in order to (a) ascertain whether the activities are taxable or not; (b) apply for an Authority to Print (ATP) for receipts or invoices and pay the Annual Registration Fee, if necessary; (c) determine the taxes due, if applicable.

3.4.	We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant be directed to prepare the necessary Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to take-up and/or effect the adjustment of the above-mentioned transactions in the books of the municipality to have a fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.
We recommended that management to re-evaluate the creation and classification of the MEE and ensure that the minimum requirements in establishing/creating an economic enterprise will be met.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management update the Revenue Code of the Municipality of Alicia pursuant to Section 191 of RA 7160 to increase the revenue collection from local taxes that can be used to finance more valuable Projects/Programs/Activities of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Management did not update the Revenue Code of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer to initiate the conduct of physical inventory taking of all properties of the municipal government and prepare and submit the corresponding RPCPPE to the audit team to establish the existence, accuracy and validity of reported balance in the financial statements.

We recommended that Demand Letters be issued to the erring officials and employees of the municipality but this time a reasonable liquidation period be set whereby the appropriate charges shall then be filed if left unattended pursuant to Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code. 

Civil actions to be initiated to run after their properties. For those still in the active service the corresponding withholding from benefits due them shall be made to apply to the unliquidated cash advances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to thoroughly review past transactions, reconcile the reciprocal accounts and effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances in order that a reliable data is presented. A copy of the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) taking up the adjustment shall be furnished the office of the Auditor

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the rules and regulations in the preparation of the financial statements. 

Also, the Municipal Accountant to revisit the provisions of the PPSAS and other COA issuances particularly on the preparation and presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Management to evaluate the strategic location of the dumpsite by considering the factors surrounding the area and its long-term effect. 

Also, to stop the operation of the open dumpsite for its garbage disposal and instead to use controlled dumpsite if sanitary landfill is not warranted considering the financial capability of the LGU.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Treasurer to enforce collection of the delinquent real property taxes by conducting tax education campaign, otherwise avail of the remedies provided in Sections 256 to 260 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the management to prioritize the completion of the necessary amenities of the local government disaster recovery facility to ensure efficient and effective response in times of calamity. 

Also, for management to coordinate with the other identified facilities for these to be provided with the necessary amenities.

We recommended that the official drivers of the Local Government and other concerned personnel to prepare and submit the Monthly Report of Official Travel and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption pursuant to COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977. 

The Monthly reports will provide a summary of trips per vehicle in chronological order based on the serially numbered Driver’s Trip Tickets.

Evaluate the staffing pattern of the local government and consider the creation of the separate GSO with staff/personnel to perform the mandated functions of the GSO; 

Require the GSO or designated personnel to submit a copy of the contracts, purchase orders and notice of delivery to the audit team within the prescribed period.

We recommended that management to comply with the provision of COA Circular No. 2013 -004 dated January 30, 2013 on Information and Publicity on PPA of government agencies, particularly on the posting of Signboards specified in Annex “A” and the general guidelines and reporting and monitoring.

We recommended that Management to comply with the requirement of the above-cited regulations for the GFPS members to be guided on the GAD policies to ensure that the programs, projects and activities of the municipality will be GAD responsive. 

Also, to establish mechanism to ensure that the GFPS are performing their mandated functions.

We recommend that Management to coordinate with the PhilHealth Regional Offices (PROs) within their respective catchment areas for the coverage of government hired JOs and PBCs under the program. 

Also, to submit justification for non-compliance with the program of PhilHealth.

We recommended that the Management to require the OIC-Municipal Accountant in coordination with the concerned officials (Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Assessor, and Municipal Engineer) to work-back the details of the beginning balance of the above-mentioned accounts. 

Also, for the ensuring years to: 

Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor to provide the schedule of the Real Property/SE Tax Receivable every reporting period;

Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to maintain a separate subsidiary ledger per fund received from NGAs and the Municipal Engineer to prepare the project cost sheet per project; and

Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to ensure that all the accounts are supported with schedule and submit the financial reports within the prescribed period.

We recommended that management shall strictly adhere to the guidelines in the granting, utilization and accounting of financial assistance to NGOs/POs as embodied in COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007 to ensure transparency and accountability of government transactions.

We recommended that the Management to allocate one percent (1%) of its Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for the implementation of the LCPC PPAs in line with the adopted child delinquency prevention plan and Annual Work and Financial Plan of the Council. 

Also, to ensure that the PPAs to be funded out of the fund are in compliance with the above-mentioned DILG Memorandum Circular in order to maximize the capability of the Municipality in addressing the issues and protecting the interest of the children in the locality.
We recommended that management adhere to the reporting requirements by preparing the required financial statement as prescribed above.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to:
Ensure that the projects will be utilized in accordance with the intended purpose to avoid its immediate deterioration and wastage of government resources.

We recommended that management refrain from authorizing deductions from the salaries of the employees that will reduce their net take home pay below P5,000.00 in 
CY 2018. Deductions from salaries should be prioritized as detailed in the GAA.

We recommended that the management to strictly follow the guidelines on the Local Roads Asset Management System provided in the COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015 in accounting and reporting all its local roads and road network system.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management to meticulously and judiciously plan the procurement process and the procurement of goods to be made through competitive bidding pursuant to Sections 7 and 10 of the revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and to refrain from paying goods on reimbursement basis without following the procurement process provided in RA 9184 and its IRR.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Assessor to prioritize the titling of the municipal lots for its protection from third party claims.

We recommended that Management initiate measures to collect the delinquent accounts from market rentals and waterworks system amounting to P668, 270.00.

The Sanggunian Bayan should enact a new tax ordinance on market and waterworks operations and to include the imposition of penalties and surcharges for non or late payment to ensure them to comply with their obligations and avoid the incurrence of delinquencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer prepare the internal guidelines for approval of the Local Chief Executive of the specific criteria, administrative procedures and standards on the grant of Magna Carta benefits to all entitled PHWs and use the new rate of the medical personnel in computing the hazard pay.

Require the Accountant to submit the Monthly and Quarterly Trial Balances and Disbursement Vouchers with the supporting documents within the prescribed period as required by existing regulations to avoid sanctions for non-compliance of the requirement and the possible institution of disciplinary action against the erring employee.

We recommended that all collections should be deposited intact daily to the Liquidating Officer and/or authorized depository bank strictly in accordance with the provision cited under Section 32 of COA Circular 2002-003 and Joint Circular 1-81 of DOF and COA.

5. We reiterated our recommendation that management  update  the  Revenue
      Code of the Municipality of Alicia pursuant to Section 191 of RA  7160
      to increase the revenue collection from local taxes that will be  used
      to  finance  more   valuable   Projects/Programs/Activities   of   the
      municipality.

8. We recommended that management grant donations after proper evaluation
      by the MSWD that determines and identifies the necessity of the grants
      to its beneficiaries.  The  Vice  Mayor  as  a  presiding  officer  of
      Sangguniang Bayan should not be allowed to extend this service to  the
      constituents in compliance with  provision  of  R.A.  No.  7160.  Said
      activity may conflict with its mandated functions as the  legislative,
      for the review and to appropriate funds  for  the  operations  of  the
      municipality.

9. We recommended that management stop the practice of  allowing  private
      vehicles  to  claim  gasoline  out  of  government  funds.  Government
      officials and employees can claim equivalent fare when  using  private
      vehicles for official travels, with the  corresponding  certification,
      and the necessary supporting documents. We further recommended for the
      refund of unnecessary claims of fuel used for these private  vehicles.
      Corresponding Notice of Disallowance will be issued when necessary.

10. We recommended that management:


     a. Strictly monitor the claims for  fuel  consumption  and  strengthen
        controls in the  withdrawal  and  consumption  of  fuel  to  ensure
        expenditures pertaining thereto are not excessive and  unnecessary.
        Also, control the issuance of two or more  government  vehicles  to
        respective drivers or individuals to maximize the use of government
        properties.


     b.  Strictly  observe  the  pertinent  rules  and  regulation  on  the
        Utilization of  Government  Motor  Vehicles  pursuant  to  the  COA
        Circular No. 77-61 known as  the  Manual  of  Fuel  Consumption  of
        Government Vehicles and in the  documentation  of  claims  required
        under Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445.


     c. Stop the practice of charging prior year’s expenditures against the
        current year’s appropriation.  Unpaid obligations shall be recorded
        as payables to validate their payments in the succeeding years.

5. We recommended management to ensure that claims for  travel  allowance
      is in accordance with the rates prescribed under Section 4 of  the  EO
      No. 298 dated March 23, 2004 as amended by EO No. 77 dated  March  15,
      2019 and should be supported with complete documentary requirements as
      required in COA  Circular  2012-001  dated  June  14,  2012.  Further,
      require the submission of certification by the agency head that  hotel
      accommodation is absolutely necessary.

6. We recommended management that:


      • Claims of  fuel  on  travel  using  government  vehicle  should  be
        supported with complete supporting  documents  in  compliance  with
        Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.


      • Only the equivalent fare  should  be  allowed  to  be  claimed  and
        reimbursed by the municipal  officials  and  employees  when  using
        private vehicles on their official travels as prescribed under  COA
        Circular No. 96-004 dated April 19, 1996.

3. We recommended that management direct the collecting officers to  stop
      the practice of encashment of checks out of  their  daily  collections
      and require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact all collections.

7. We recommended that management establish and book up the RPT  and  SET
      Receivables accounts  at the  beginning  of  each  year  pursuant  to
      Sections 19(b) and 20 of NGAS Manual for LGUs.

4. We recommended management to comply with Section 2.2.6 to 2.2.8 of COA
      Circular No. 2013-004 and DILG Memorandum Circular  No.  2010-101  and
      refrain from placing unnecessary marks on vehicles purchased and other
      projects and programs undertaken.

6. We recommended that management  strictly  adhere  with  the  mandatory
      requirement of public bidding in all  transactions  not  exempt  under
      Rule XVI of the IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

4. We recommended that management record / recognize the  deduction  made
      for rental payment from the cost of feeder line  installation  in  the
      books of the agency.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) directs the Municipal Treasurer/GSO in coordination with the committee members to; (a) initiate the conduct of physical inventory and/or verification of all properties of the municipal government; and (b) prepare and submit the corresponding RPCPPE to the audit team to establish the existence, accuracy and validity of reported balance of the PPE in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be directed to take-up the money value of the earned leave credits of the officers and employees of the municipality to have a fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant be directed to prepare the necessary Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to take-up and/or effect the adjustment of the above-mentioned transactions in the books of the municipality to have a fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be directed to thoroughly review past transactions, reconcile the reciprocal accounts and effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances in order that a reliable data is presented. A copy of the JEVs taking up the adjustment shall be furnished the office of the Auditor.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant be directed to (a) prepare the necessary adjusting JEVs to reclassify the accounts; and (b) to establish mechanism to ensure the transactions is recorded in an appropriate account classification.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant be directed to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the details of the Loans Payable – Domestic account to provide clarity and divulge information that are not readily available on the face of the financial statements as prescribed in IPSAS No. 1.

We recommended that the LCE directs the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the rules and regulations on the preparation of the financial statements. Also, the Municipal Accountant to revisit the provisions of the IPSAS and other COA issuances particularly on the preparation and presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended management to issue final demand letter to the concerned AOs including those separated, resigned, transferred and retired from service.  Failure of the AO to settle their outstanding cash advance within the period specified in the final demand letter, we recommend that management take appropriate remedial actions provided by the laws, rules and regulations.

We further recommended that in cases where the conditions provided for in the COA Circular No. 2016-005 are met, the LCE shall file the request for authority to write-off the dormant unliquidated cash advances to the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/or Supervising Auditor (SA) following the procedures and specific guidelines set forth in Paragraph 8.0 of the said Circular.

We recommended that Management submit a written explanation (a) for the very low percentage of implemented projects under the 20% EDF. Also, we reiterate our previous recommendation that the LCE to prioritize the implementation of the development projects so that the expected output/outcome of the projects will be attained. Moreover, ensure that Municipal Planning and Development Office and Municipal Engineering Office regularly monitor the status of project implementation so that necessary interventions may be introduced at the earliest time.

We recommended that management submit the documents pertaining to all suspensions and/or time extensions of the above-mentioned projects.
Moving forward, we recommended that management: (a) require the personnel of the Municipal Engineering Office to conduct regular monitoring and supervision of projects to ensure that infrastructure projects are accomplished in accordance with the terms of the contract and document the same; and (b) impose liquidated damages and other remedies provided by the rules and regulations for any unjustified violation of the terms and conditions of the contract.

We recommended that the LCE requires the Municipal Engineering Office to immediately submit the pertinent documents of the above projects to facilitate the conduct of the technical review and/or inspections. Also, we recommended that management institute measures to ensure strict compliance with the above-mentioned regulations.
We recommended that the BAC institute mechanism to ensure the compliance of the procurement activities with the provisions of the RA No. 9184 and its RIRR.

We recommended that management require: (a) the compliance of the documentary requirements prescribed in Item 3.1.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 before the funds will be release to the concerned barangay; and (b) the recipient barangays to submit the report on the utilization (liquidation report) of the funds.

We recommended that Management comply with the requirement of the above-cited regulations for the GFPS members to be guided on the GAD policies to ensure that the programs, projects and activities of the municipality will be GAD responsive. Also, to establish mechanism to ensure that the GFPS are performing their mandated functions.

We recommended that management comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013 -004 dated January 30, 2013 on Information and Publicity on PPA of government agencies, particularly on the posting of Signboards specified in Annex “A” and the general guidelines, and reporting and monitoring. Also, to regularly update the status of the PPAs in the signboards.

We recommend that the municipal treasurer and the municipal assessor prepare data on real property through a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible from the Taxpayer’s index card. A copy of the list must be submitted to the municipal accountant for the preparation of the necessary accounting entries to set-up the actual Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivables of the municipality.

We recommend that management make representations to the source agency - Department of Environment and Natural Resources (DENR) on the release and turn-over of these projects to the agency/organizations who are responsible for the proper operation and maintenance of the structures.

We also recommend that the municipal accountant prepare adjusting journal entries to drop the cost of the assets that are not owned by the municipality from its books after due notice from the DENR on the turn-over in accordance with paragraph 7 of the International  Public  Sector  Accounting  Standard (IPSAS) 1.

We recommend that the General Services Officer or the Local Treasurer, as the case maybe, initiate the preparation of stock/property cards for new purchases.

We further recommend that management require the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of the property, plant and equipment and submit copies of the reports to the Municipal Accountant for the proper recording of adjustments and to the Auditor for verification.

We recommend that management requests the Development Bank of the Philippines (DBP), through a written communication, to transfer the restriction or the hold-out to the cash in bank account under the general fund. Otherwise, management has to allocate and transfer funds from the general fund to trust fund in accordance with Section 308(b) of the Local Government Code.

We recommend that management send a show cause order to the contractor for them to explain the delay and to fast track project implementation. Further, we recommend that the municipal accountant deduct liquidated damages on the final claim of the contractor.

We also recommend that follow-up on project implementation in the succeeding years be done through written communications with the contractors and ensure that liquidated damages be deducted for the delayed implementation of the projects.

We recommend that the concerned personnel submit written explanation on why the Tourism Information Center was left idle, unsecured and not properly maintained after it was completed in CY 2016.

We further recommend that management create policies on the use and maintenance of the building to ensure that the Tourism Information Center be utilized as intended and duly safeguarded in accordance with Sections 375(c) and 444(b)(3)(vii) of the Local Government Code.

We recommend that management ensure that these allocations are not included in the 20% Development Fund in the succeeding years.

We further recommend that management observes the provision in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 in the identification of allowable projects for the 20% Development Fund.

We recommend that management submit written justification on the inclusion of BAC Honorarium in the annual budget. Otherwise, irregular payment of honorarium will be disallowed in audit.

We also recommend that the municipal budget officer ensure that the composition of the budget are in accordance with prescribed laws, rules and regulations to avoid irregular disbursements of funds.

We recommend that Management submit justification for the non-creation of all the mandatory positions of the Local DRRM Office.

Further, we recommend that the Management initiate the creation of the two required positions and request the Sangguniang Bayan to pass a Resolution creating the same in accordance to Section 12 of RA 10121.

We recommend that management require the submission of the following documents in addition to the documents required in the procurement:
Acknowledgement from the department head that the office have received the number of sacks of rice
Certified list of personnel entitled to the commodity
Rosters of inmates duly certified by the Jail Warden/Superintendent
Certification that the personnel have not received the same benefit from any other government agencies

We recommend that Management require the concerned municipal officials/employee to immediately submit the deficient financial reports and ensure that the prescribed periods in the submission of reports are observed. Failure to submit these reports on time shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries pursuant to Sections 122 of PD 1445.

We recommend that management make appropriate revision of the GAD Plan and Budget in the succeeding years and ensure that the total cost of the related activities is at least 5% of the total budget appropriations.

We recommend that management revisit the Revenue Code of the municipality and revise the fees imposed on the sale of bidding documents in accordance with the Guidelines on the Sale of Bidding Documents, Appendix 8 on the Handbook on Philippine Government Procurement.

We recommended that management properly estimate income to maximize programming of projects, program and activities for the benefit of the constituents of the Municipality and make an assessment of the collection efficiency from time to time as a basis to intensify collection activities in order to meet the estimated revenue collection target.
observations.managementAction: Management was able to properly estimate income to and make timely assessment of its collection efficiency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management monitor and ensure the implementation and completion of all the LGU’s programs, projects and activities funded under the 20% Development Fund to immediately serve the intended purpose/s and the use thereof by the constituents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management to provide adequate and safe shelter for storage of supplies, parking space of the vehicle/equipment to safeguard and to prolong the useful life of the assets.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to prepare the monthly BRS and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period for review and verification.

We recommended that management to strictly adhere with the rules and regulations on the CA as provided in Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
Likewise, the Municipal Accounting Office to ensure that the disbursement vouchers are duly supported with complete documentary requirements before processing the transaction for payment.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the guidelines on the preparation and presentation of the Financial Statements specifically on the submission of a complete sets of financial statements.
We recommended that the Management to institute measure to ensure that priority development projects will be implemented on time so that the expected output/outcome of the projects will be attained.

We recommended that the management to ensure that the report is prepared in accordance with the prescribed format and content.

We recommended that management to re-evaluate the creation and classification of the MEE and ensure that the minimum requirements in establishing/creating an economic enterprise will be met.

We recommended that Management require the LDRRMO to prioritize and implement the programs/projects/activities identified during the year to ensure the readiness of the Municipality in case of calamity and to avoid the conduct of similar activities every budget year.
We reiterate our previous recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer to return to the source agencies any balance of the fund received for all completed projects.

We recommended that the management to revisit the policy on the grant of mobile load allowance considering its economy and efficient use of resources in the performance of services and to stop granting internet load allowance to its officials.

Conduct periodic reconciliation of records in the ensuing periods to ensure the reliability of records and in compliance with Section 74 of PD 1445.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries in the General Fund to close the Trust Liability - DRRMF and credit the Government Equity account in order to reflect the correct balances in the financial statements. Further, ensures that any reversion of the LDRRMF from Trust Fund to the General Fund in the succeeding years be recorded as an adjustment to Government Equity and not as a liability. 

We also recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer include in the Status of Appropriation, Allotment and Obligation (SAAOB) and in the budget execution the reverted amount per supplemental budget, pending utilization thereof.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management update the Revenue Code of the
    Municipality of Alicia pursuant to Section 191 of RA 7160 to  increase
    the revenue collection from local taxes that can be used to finance more
    valuable Projects/Programs/Activities of the municipality.

We recommended that management  properly  estimate  income  to  maximize
    programming of projects, program and activities for the benefit  of  the
    constituents  of  the  Municipality  and  make  an  assessment  of   the
    collection efficiency  from  time  to  time  as  a  basis  to  intensify
    collection activities in order to meet the estimated revenue  collection
    target.

We recommended that management monitor and ensure the implementation and
    completion of all the LGU’s programs,  projects  and  activities  funded
    under the  20%  Development  Fund  to  immediately  serve  the  intended
    purpose/s and the use thereof by the constituents.

We recommended management  to  collect  from  the  Robinsons  Supermarket
   Corporation what  is  due  for  the  government  and  execute  the  lease
   agreement for the space  being  occupied  by  them.    Proper  adjustment
   should be made in the books of the  municipality  for  the  corresponding
   rental income for the month of December while the  security  deposit  and
   advance rental should be collected as soon as possible.

We recommended that management properly classify  the  completed  Alicia
   Commercial Building to Investment Property Account as define in the PPSAS
   instead of classifying it as Building since it was constructed as  income
   generating property for leasing to interested third parties.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer to initiate the conduct  of
physical inventory taking of all properties of the municipal government  and
prepare and submit the corresponding RPCPPE to the audit team  to  establish
the existence, accuracy and validity of reported balance  in  the  financial
statements.

      We recommend that the management to provide adequate and safe  shelter
for  storage  of  supplies,  parking  space  of  the  vehicle/equipment   to
safeguard and to prolong the useful life of the assets.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to prepare the monthly  BRS
and submit the same to the Office  of  the  Auditor  within  the  prescribed
period for review and verification.

We recommend that Demand Letters be issued  to  the  erring  officials
and employees of the municipality but this  time  a  reasonable  liquidation
period be set whereby the appropriate charges shall then be  filed  if  left
unattended pursuant to Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code. Civil  actions
to be initiated to run after  their  properties.  For  those  still  in  the
active service the corresponding withholding from benefits  due  them  shall
be made to apply to the unliquidated cash advances.

      Henceforth, the granting of CAs shall be in  accordance  with  of  COA
Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997

We recommend that management to strictly adhere  with  the  rules  and
regulations on the CA as provided in Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA  Circular
No. 97-002 dated February  10,  1997.  Likewise,  the  Municipal  Accounting
Office to ensure that the disbursement  vouchers  are  duly  supported  with
complete documentary requirements  before  processing  the  transaction  for
payment.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to thoroughly  review  past
transactions, reconcile the reciprocal accounts and  effect  adjustments  to
reflect the correct account balances  in  order  that  a  reliable  data  is
presented. A copy  of  the  Journal  Entry  Vouchers  (JEV)  taking  up  the
adjustment shall be furnished the office of the Auditor.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant  to  strictly  observe  the
guidelines on the preparation and presentation of the  Financial  Statements
specifically on the submission of a complete sets of financial statements.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to  direct  the  Municipal
Accountant  to  strictly  observe  the  rules  and  regulations   i67n   the
preparation of the financial statements. Also, the Municipal  Accountant  to
revisit the provisions of the PPSAS and other COA issuances particularly  on
the preparation and presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommend that the Management to evaluate  the  strategic  location
of the dumpsite by considering the factors  surrounding  the  area  and  its
long term effect. Also, to stop the operation of the open dumpsite  for  its
garbage  disposal  and  instead  to  use  controlled  dumpsite  if  sanitary
landfill is not warranted considering the financial capability of the LGU.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to require  the  Municipal
Treasurer to enforce collection of the delinquent  real  property  taxes  by
conducting tax education campaign, otherwise avail of the remedies  provided
in Sections 256 to 260 of RA No. 7160.

We recommend that management to:


     a. Conduct thorough evaluation and review of the  municipalitys  need
        for hiring casuals, contractual and JO employees;
     b. Ensure compliance with the provisions of CSC resolutions to  ensure
        protection of government funds from unnecessary expenses; and
     c.  Require  the  HRMO  to  evaluate  the  staffing  pattern  of   the
        Municipality in order to limit the hiring  of  casual,  contractual
        and job order employees and be guided by the provisions of the  CSC
        resolutions.

We recommend that the Management to institute measure to  ensure  that
priority development projects will  be  implemented  on  time  so  that  the
expected output/outcome of the projects will be attained.

We recommend that the management to prioritize the completion  of  the
necessary amenities of the local government disaster  recovery  facility  to
ensure efficient and effective response in  times  of  calamity.  Also,  for
management to coordinate with the other identified facilities for  these  to
be provided with the necessary amenities.

We recommend  that  the  management  to  ensure  that  the  report  is
prepared in accordance with the prescribed  format  and  content.  Also,  to
submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within  the  prescribed  period
in accordance with the above-cited regulations.

We  recommend  that  management  to  re-evaluate  the  creation   and
classification of the MEE  and  ensure  that  the  minimum  requirements  in
establishing/creating an economic enterprise will be met.

We recommend that the official drivers of  the  Local  Government  and
other concerned personnel to  prepare  and  submit  the  Monthly  Report  of
Official Travel and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption pursuant  to  COA
Circular No. 77-61 dated  September  26,  1977.  The  Monthly  reports  will
provide a summary of trips per vehicle in chronological order based  on  the
serially numbered Drivers Trip Tickets.

We recommend that management to:


     a. Evaluate the staffing pattern of the local government and  consider
        the creation of the separate GSO with  staff/personnel  to  perform
        the mandated functions of the GSO; and
     b. Require the GSO or designated personnel to submit  a  copy  of  the
        contracts, purchase orders and notice of delivery to the audit team
        within the prescribed period.

We recommend that the General Services Officer or the Local Treasurer, as the case maybe, prepares stock/property cards and that the Municipal Accountant also maintain separate ledger cards to establish the details of the assets.
We further recommend that management require the Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of the property, plant and equipment and submit copies of the reports to the Municipal Accountant for the proper recording of adjustments and to the Auditor for verification.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries in the General Fund to close the Trust Liability - DRRMF and credit the Government Equity account in order to reflect the correct balances in the financial statements. Further, ensures that any reversion of the LDRRMF from Trust Fund to the General Fund in the succeeding years be recorded as an adjustment to Government Equity and not as a liability.
We also recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer include in the Status of Appropriation, Allotment and Obligation (SAAOB) and in the budget execution the reverted amount per supplemental budget, pending utilization thereof.

We recommend that management require the LDRRM Officer to promptly submit the monthly Report of Sources and Utilization of DRRMF, duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant, on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to ascertain whether or not these payable accounts under the Trust Fund are still due for remittance to concerned offices.  If these are still due for remittance, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to remit immediately the amount involved in order to protect the interest of the employee-members concerned and clear the books of these prior years’ payable accounts.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant ensures that only the current year’s unexpended LDRRMF will be recorded as transfers at year-end in order to present reliable Surplus/Net Income. If the accounting entries were not prepared at year-end, prior year’s unexpended LDRRMF shall be recorded as Prior Period Adjustments in the succeeding year.

We recommend that Municipal Accountant adjust the subsidiary ledgers of two (2) fund transfers to reflect the actual implementation and liquidation of the Assistance to Disadvantaged Municipalities (ADM) Project and prepare communication to the source agency informing them that there was an error in the Liquidation Report submitted.

We recommend that management fast track the procurement and the implementation of the infrastructure projects funded by the Local Government Support Fund – Assistance to Municipalities (LGSF-AM) to maximize the benefits that could address the needs of the people in the community in consonance with the DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2018-61.

We recommend that management prioritize the implementation of the projects funded by the 20% Development Fund.  Further, ensure that identified projects are implemented within the year in order to deliver the intended benefits and to get the maximum value of the fund considering the increasing prices of materials and labor costs.

We recommend that management submit justification on the late submission of perfected contracts and further require the BAC Chairman and the Secretariat to furnish copies of the perfected contracts/purchase orders with complete supporting documents within five (5) days after approval. Any unjustified failure of the concerned officials and employees to comply shall be subjected to the administrative disciplinary action under the penalty clause in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommend that management make appropriate revision of the GAD Plan and Budget in the succeeding years and ensure that the total cost of the related activities is at least 5% of the total budget appropriations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management  (a)  strictly  observe/  follow  the
       updated description of accounts as prescribed under COA Circular No.
       2013-002  January  30,  2013;  (b)  observe  pertinent   rules   and
       regulations on the Utilization of Government Motor Vehicles pursuant
       to  the  COA  Circular  No.  77-61  known  as  the  Manual  of  Fuel
       Consumption of Government  Vehicles  and  in  the  documentation  of
       claims required under section 4 (6) of P.D. No. 1445.; (c)  strictly
       monitored claims for fuel consumptions.

We recommended that management require the accountant to ensure that
       claims for travelling  is  in  accordance  with  Section  4  of  the
       Executive Order (EO) No.298 dated March 23, 2004.


    Management  was  also  advised  to  refrain  from  reimbursing  fuel
       expenses  on   the   claim   on   travel   expenses   whenever   the
       official/employee   used   their    private    vehicle.    If    the
       official/employee used their private  vehicle  only  the  equivalent
       fair should be allowed.


    All claims on travel expenses  should  be  supported  with  complete
       documentary requirements as required in COA Circular 2012-001  dated
       June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the Management ensure that the  20%  Development
       Fund of the LGU be allotted and be  used  for  priority  development
       programs, projects and activities as prescribed under DILG-DBM Joint
       Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.


    As regards the expenditures amounting to ₱821,392.45, management  is
       required to submit an explanation  and/or  justification  why  these
       were charged against the 20% Development Fund and not to  the  usual
       Repair and Maintenance account.

We  recommended  that  Management  should  monitor  and  ensure  the
       implementation and completion of all the  LGU’s  programs,  projects
       and activities funded under the 20% Development Fund to  immediately
       serve the intended purpose or use thereof to the constituents.

We recommended management to allocate the required 1% of IRA for the
       LCPC as provided under Section 15 of R.A. 9344, and  to  direct  the
       members of the LCPC to prepare Local Development Plan  for  Children
       and Local Investment Plan for Children, and maximize utilization  of
       the allocated LCPC Fund to the identified PPAs provided for in  DILG
       MC No 2012-120, to ensure full strengthening and  implementation  of
       the LCPC.

We recommended management to  adhere  with  the  provisions  of  COA
       Circular Nos. 76-34 and 2009-001.  Submission of perfected contracts
       (Purchase Orders, Contracts) to the Office of the Audit Team  Leader
       must be strictly complied with, for  proper  review  and  evaluation
       thereof.

We recommended that management prior to the approval  of  employee’s
       loans, there should be a verification of the latest  net  take  home
       pay of the employee for  guidance  of  the  approving  authority  in
       determining whether such loan should be approved or not  and  comply
       with the general provisions of the GAA on the  minimum  allowed  net
       take home pay of all government employee.

We recommended management to  adhere  with  the  provisions  of  COA
       Circular No. 75-6 and ensure that all vehicles and  equipment  owned
       by the City be marked with “For Official Use Only” followed  by  the
       name of the Municipality of Alicia to safeguard government  property
       against potential mismanagement and misuse.

We recommended the management to require the Municipal Treasurer  to
       stamp “PAID” the Disbursement  Vouchers  and  all  their  supporting
       documents after payment to avoid the  use  of  the  same  supporting
       documents for future  claims  in  accordance  with  Item  2  of  COA
       Circular 92-389 dated November 3, 1992 to  maintain  sound  internal
       control in the disbursement of government funds.

We recommended that the project funded  by  the  secured  loan  with
       Robinsons Bank be completed so that necessary fund for  the  payment
       of the forthcoming principal repayments on the loan  can  be  funded
       out of the  generated  income  in  this  project.   Monitor  and  if
       necessary stop approving suspensions of work and grant of extensions
       to the contractor for the advantage of the municipality.

We  recommend  that  concerned  management  officials  to  religiously
implement the above cited regulations on the conduct  of  periodic  physical
inventory of the LGU’s PPE.

We  recommend  that  the  Management  to  require  the   OIC-Municipal
Accountant  in  coordination  with  the   concerned   officials   (Municipal
Treasurer, Municipal Assessor, and  Municipal  Engineer)  to  work-back  the
details of the beginning balance of the above-mentioned accounts. Also,  for
the ensuring years to:


     a. Require the Municipal  Treasurer  and  the  Municipal  Assessor  to
        provide the schedule of the Real Property/SE Tax  Receivable  every
        reporting period;



     b.  Require  the  OIC-Municipal  Accountant  to  maintain  a  separate
        subsidiary ledger per fund received from  NGAs  and  the  Municipal
        Engineer to prepare the project cost sheet per project; and

     c. Require  the  OIC-Municipal  Accountant  to  ensure  that  all  the
        accounts are supported  with  schedule  and  submit  the  financial
        reports within the prescribed period.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the  monthly
BRS are prepared in accordance with the above-cited regulations.

We recommend that management to:

     a. Require all the officials and employees  to  liquidate  their  cash
        advances immediately by issuing demand letters;

     b. Institute appropriate legal sanction against concerned officials or
        employees, by withholding or suspension of their salaries;

     c. Exert all efforts to  expedite  liquidation  of  cash  advances  of
        officers and employees who  have  already  died  or  no  longer  in
        service. After exhausting efforts to have  these  accounts  settled
        and no other alternative procedures are available,  management  may
        file a request for write-off (except  for  the  cash  advances  for
        Confidential Expenses) in accordance with the provision of  Section
        4 of Rule VIII, 2009 Revised Rules and Provisions of the Commission
        on Audit dated September 15, 2009; and

     d. Comply with the requirements set forth under COA Circular No. 2016-
        005 dated December 19, 2016

We recommend that Management direct:

in the granting, utilization  and  accounting  of  financial  assistance  to
NGOs/POs as embodied in COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007  to
ensure transparency and accountability of government transactions.

necessary adjusting Journal  Entry  Vouchers  to  reclassify  the  accounts.

reporting periods.

prioritize the preparation of documents to speed-up  the  implementation  of
these projects so that the expected output/outcome of the projects  will  be
attained. Further for management to adopt measures and  strategies  to  fast
track the implementation of projects.

We recommend that Management require  the  LDRRMO  to  prioritize  and
implement the programs/projects/activities identified  during  the  year  to
ensure the readiness of the Municipality in case of calamity  and  to  avoid
the conduct of similar activities every budget year.

We recommend that the Management to prioritize the  implementation  of
the  development  projects  so  that  the  expected  output/outcome  of  the
projects will be attained.

We recommend that the management to ensure compliance with the  above-
cited regulation in appropriating  and  utilizing  the  20%  EDF.  Also,  to
realign the remaining unimplemented maintenance and  operating  expenditures
funded from the 20% EDF.

We recommend that the Management to allocate one percent (1%)  of  its
Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for the implementation of the LCPC PPAs  in
line with the adopted child delinquency prevention plan and Annual Work  and
Financial Plan of the Council. Also, to ensure that the PPAs  to  be  funded
out of the fund are in compliance with the above-mentioned  DILG  Memorandum
Circular in  order  to  maximize  the  capability  of  the  Municipality  in
addressing the issues and protecting the interest of  the  children  in  the
locality.

We recommend that management adhere to the reporting  requirements  by
preparing the required financial statement as prescribed above.

We recommend  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  instruct  the  Local
Economic Enterprise Officer to prepare the Five (5)-Year Business  Plan  for
each LEE.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to:


     a. Ensure the immediate completion of the project; and


     b. Ensure that the projects will be utilized in  accordance  with  the
        intended purpose to avoid its immediate deterioration  and  wastage
        of government resources.

We recommend that the concerned officials and  employees  to  strictly
comply  with  the  above-mentioned  regulation.  Also,  for  management   to
identify the cause of the  non-submission  and  to  institute  the  sanction
provided in the penalty clause to the officials and employees concerned.

We recommend that Management require the Treasurer to find ways on how
to facilitate the preparation and submission  of  the  duly  certified  list
showing the names of all taxpayers and the tax due  from  each  taxpayer  at
the start of the year to the Accountant as  a  basis  of  recording  in  the
books of accounts the RPT/SET Receivables.  This  way,  the  actual  RPT/SET
Receivables every beginning of each  year  is reflected  in  the  books  of
accounts. Moreover, the Accountant should make a detailed  analysis  of  the
Receivable accounts and their contra accounts to determine  the  cause/s  of
the discrepancies and effect the  necessary  adjustments  in  the  books  of
accounts.

will shelter these LGU acquired vehicles  away  from  the  natural  elements
that will prolong its useful life.  The Management may  also  transfer  them
to other area so as to keep the properties from theft or misuse.

the salaries of the employees that will  reduce  their  net  take  home  pay
below P5,000.00 in
