observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the LDRRMO to furnish a copy of the LDRRMF-DRRMF-MOOE and Investment Plan to the OIC Municipal Accountant for the recording of the unexpended CYs 2011-2015 Quick Response Fund and DRRMF-MOOE in the Trust Fund as Trust Liability, and to the Budget Officer for the recording of the DRRMF-Capital Outlay as continuing appropriation in the SAAOB. Require the OIC Municipal Treasurer to issue check to actually set aside and transfer to the Trust Fund the unexpended LDRRMF (QRF & MOOE) for CY 2011-2015. Advise the Sanggunian to revert back to the General Fund the unexpended balance of the CY 2010 LDRRMF and identify projects/programs for the available funds.
observations.managementAction: AAR The unexpended balance was transferred to the Trust Fund in February 2017. The unexpended balances of the CY 2010-2011 was reverted back to the general fund with the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan. The DRRMF-MOOE were recorded in the Trust Fund as Trust Liability and the DRRMF-Capital Outlay as continuing appropriation in the SAAOB.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the LDRRMO to submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) at the start of the year and the monthly Report of Sources and Utilization of DRRMF duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
observations.managementAction: AAR Management submits LDRRM Reports at year-end.
observations.reason: There is only one permanent employee under the MDRRMO. (Reiterated in Finding No. 15)

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require all officials and employees concerned to submit immediately the liquidation reports of their outstanding cash advances and/or refund the unexpended cash balance, if any. Otherwise, direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately withhold the payment of their salaries or any amount due them.
observations.managementAction: AAR A memorandum was issued to all offices/departments regarding the liquidation of cash advances. At year end, only the Confidential Fund of P200,000.00 remained in the books since no credit notice from ICFAU was received.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management request approval from the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF) to maintain accounts with banks not compliant with the requirements by following Section 5.5 of the DOF Department Circular 001-2015.
observations.managementAction: AAR The requirements to secure the approval of the BLGF to maintain the FCB account was submitted in the first quarter of CY 2017. The FCB Guindulman had a balance of P12,768.09 as of December 31, 2017.
observations.reason: Follow-ups has been made but no response. A new account was opened at DBP-Ubay in replacement of the FCB account.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the BAC Chairman and the Secretariat to furnish copies of the perfected contracts/purchase orders with complete supporting documents within five (5) days after approval. Any unjustified failure of the concerned officials and employees to comply shall be subjected to the administrative disciplinary action under the penalty clause in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: AAR Some copies of the contracts/purchase orders were submitted on time while others were delayed
observations.reason: Due to the increase number of transactions and projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to submit monthly trial balances on the 20th day of the following month to facilitate the verification of transactions and reports of the LGU, as well as submit quarterly trial balance and year-end reports on time.
observations.managementAction: AAR The Municipal Accountant prepared the year-end reports. However, submission was delayed. The personnel assigned to help the OIC – Municipal Accountant in the preparation of reports was on leave and didn’t return to work after her labor/delivery. The OIC – Municipal Accountant was only able to find a replacement at the end of October, 2017.
observations.reason: The assigned personnel have been busy with other function.

observations.recommendation: Require the inventory committee to conduct a complete physical count of all properties and submit the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) and Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Auditor in accordance with Section 124 of the Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS), Volume I. Likewise, reconciliation between the records maintained by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant should be made and effect appropriate adjustments when necessary.
observations.managementAction: AAR The report of the physical count in CY 2014 was submitted to the auditor on April 17, 2016. The report of the physical count in CY 2015 was also not submitted on time. The report of the physical count in CY 2016 and 2017 was not submitted.
observations.reason: The report of the physical count in CY 2016 and 2017 was not submitted. (Reiterated in Finding No. 1)

observations.recommendation: In view of the SC’s decision on the unconstitutionality of the PDAF, we recommend that the local government unit return the PDAF balance to the DSWD for reversion to the unappropriated surplus in the general fund of the national government in accordance with the Supreme Court decision dated November 19, 2013.
observations.managementAction: AAR The amount of P202,847.00 was utilized, while the amount of P489.00 was returned to DSWD.
observations.reason: P64,097.81 are old accounts, still subject for verification on where to return the balance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the OIC- Municipal Accountant prepare the report and submit as soon as possible its Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation for the AAR 2013. It is further recommended that management comply with the provision and submit the action plan within sixty (60) days upon receipt of the AAR 2014 and its ensuing years.
observations.managementAction: AAR The Municipal Accountant prepared the AAPSI. However, submission was delayed.
observations.reason: The assigned personnel have been busy with other function.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Accountant to record purchases of supplies and materials to the inventory account. Require the Municipal Treasurer or concerned department/office to maintain complete records, to include Stock Cards and Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued, (SSMI) showing the receipt and issuances of supplies and materials; Require the Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer to furnish the SSMI to the Accounting Unit to record its issuances; Require the Accounting Unit to record issuances based on the SSMI submitted; Require the Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer to reconcile balances of the stock cards with the subsidiary ledgers at the accounting office.
observations.managementAction: AAR Procured/purchased supplies & materials were recorded using the Stock Cards and Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) were not maintained by the Municipal Treasurer or GSO. No SSMI prepared for submission to the Accounting Office. Issuance was based on the acceptance report of the requesting unit. No reconciliation made.
observations.reason: Complete records which include Stock Cards and Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) were not maintained by the Municipal Treasurer or GSO. No assigned personnel to do the reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the drivers concerned to serially number the trip tickets and properly fill up all pertinent information thereof; Appoint/designate an employee to strictly monitor the utilization of vehicles and to submit the required Monthly Report on Official Travels and Monthly Report on Fuel Consumption to evaluate the proper use of all transportations and fuel consumption to prevent possible wastage of funds.
observations.managementAction: AAR Monthly Reports of Official Travels and Report of Fuel Consumption were prepared. However, the designated employee may not be able to furnish the Auditor with the copies of the reports.
observations.reason: Instead of consolidating the official travels and fuel consumptions of all government vehicles in one month, reports were made per government-owned vehicle which were attached to the DVs.

observations.recommendation: Require the municipal treasurer at the beginning of the year to prepare the certified list of taxpayer with the amount due and collectible for the year and submit to the municipal accountant for recording. Require the municipal accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the debit to Real Property Tax Receivable/Special Education Tax Receivable and the credit to Deferred Real Property Tax Income/Deferred Special Education Tax Income based on the list submitted by the municipal treasurer. Require the municipal treasurer to prepare a current list of delinquent taxpayers with the amount due and collectible and submit to the municipal accountant for reconciliation of records to establish a correct balance of RPT/SET Receivables. Require the municipal accountant to make the necessary adjustment in the books based on the correct RPT/SET balance.
observations.managementAction: AAR A certified list of taxpayers had been submitted for recording. Based on the certified list a journal entry voucher was drawn to set up the RPT/SET receivable for CY 2014. The Municipal Treasurer had submitted a current list of delinquent taxpayers which was used as basis to adjust the RPT/SET receivable balance.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of records is still on process due to sizeable number of taxpayers.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management effect the proper liquidation of the fund transfers to the concerned agencies by returning the remaining balances of the funds downloaded for the execution of the projects; Use the funds received in accordance with the project proposals on a timely manner to maximize the beneficial use of the projects by the people; Require the municipal accountant to reclassify the Kilusang Kabuhayan at Kaunlaran (KKK) loan receivables to Other Receivable account; Require the municipal account to verify what agencies the fund balances belong and once ascertained return the excess funds or request an authority from the concerned agencies to retain the fund for other purposes.
observations.managementAction: AAR Liquidations of some funds were submitted. Projects from fund transfers were partially implemented due to time constraint. For reclassification no reclassification made. Confirmation to the issuing agency on refund or request for authority to use the fund was not made.
observations.reason: This is due to the increase number of transactions and projects. The year-end reports were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the unserviceable assets to Other Assets account in the books of the municipality; Require the Disposal/Inventory Committee to dispose immediately the damaged/unserviceable assets; Require the Municipal Accountant to effect adjustments in the books based on the disposed properties.
observations.managementAction: AAR No disposal was made.
observations.reason: Records for disposal not yet completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update Supplies Ledger Cards (SLC) and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) to support the accuracy of the balances of the general ledger and as a control mechanism in the receipts and issuances of office supplies and equipment; Require the reconciliation of the Property Cards and the Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) of the Municipal Treasurer’s Office with the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) of the Accounting Unit.
observations.managementAction: AAR The accounting section had maintained the updated SLC & PPELC records. No assigned personnel to do the reconciliation. No reconciliation made between the Property Cards and the Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) of the Municipal Treasurer’s Office with the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) of the Accounting Unit.
observations.reason: No assigned personnel to do the reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to ensure prompt and full implementation of the developmental projects been programmed for implementation during the current year in order to achieve the intended purposes for which the fund was appropriated.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2012 projects were already implemented.
observations.reason: Current year’s 20% Development Fund were not fully implemented since the LGU is also the implementing agency of several projects of the national government.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to effect immediately all reconciling items in the respective records to put in order the balances of accounts in the financial statements. Conduct periodic reconciliation of records in the ensuing periods to ensure the reliability of records and in compliance with Section 74 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: No action
observations.reason: The OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer did not adjust the reconciling items and no reconciliation was made between their records.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our recommendation that management, in accordance with Item 9.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-004 suspend the salary of the accountable officers still employed in the agency who failed to liquidate to date their outstanding cash advances as of December 31, 2011 and request for write-off for those accountable officer who had already died.
observations.managementAction:
Issued demand letter

No additional cash advance (CA) was granted to AO with unliquidated CA. Travelling expenses were made on reimbursement basis.
observations.reason: Reiterated in 2016 AAR

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that:

1.	The Accountant, before effecting payment on the procurement of drugs and medicines, to require the submission of certifications/documents as provided for in COA Circular No. 2012 – 001 and RA No. 9502

2.	The BAC-TWG to require during post-qualification, the submission of Certificate of Product Registration of each drug/medicine being procured to ensure that these are duly registered with the DOH-FDA.

3.	The Municipal Health Officer to submit a certification that the drugs and medicines being procured are based on the PNDF Vol. I, current edition, in full compliance with RA No. 9502 and IRR.
observations.managementAction: No action
observations.reason: Procurement of drugs and medicines for CY 2016 was suspended in audit due to lack of supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: The Accountant and the Property Officer should require the end users of the gasoline, oil, and lubricants to prepare and submit duly accomplished the driver’s trip ticket, report of official travel and monthly report of fuel consumption for verification as to the reasonableness of fuel consumption.
observations.managementAction: Some official travels were supported with driver’s trip ticket.
observations.reason: Report of fuel consumption supported with the driver’s trip ticket and the report of official travels still not submitted by management to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Health Officer prepare the internal guidelines for approval of the Local Chief Executive of the specific criteria, administrative procedures and standards on the grant of Magna Carta benefits to all entitled PHWs and use the new rate of the medical personnel in computing the hazard pay.
observations.managementAction: No action
observations.reason: No internal guidelines on the grant of hazard pay.

observations.recommendation: We recommend for meticulous and judicious procurement planning and the procurement of goods through competitive bidding pursuant to Sections 7 and 10 of the revised IRR of RA No. 9184. Refrain from paying procurement of goods on reimbursement basis.
observations.managementAction: The management instructed all department heads and Bids & Award Committee to abide with RA 9184.
observations.reason: Reiterated in 2016 AAR

observations.recommendation: The Local Chief Executive should instruct the Municipal Engineer to display notification so that the public will be properly informed in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular 2013-004.
observations.managementAction: No action
observations.reason: Reiterated in 2016 AAR

observations.recommendation:
Stop operating in an open dumpsite for its garbage disposal instead sanitary landfill should be developed as final garbage disposal site for the LGU.
Management should give priority attention in the preparation and approval of its Local Government Ecological Solid Waste Management Plan as required under RA 9003.
observations.managementAction: Solid Waste Management Plan has been formulated and submitted but not yet approved.
observations.reason: Dumpsite is not covered but controlled

observations.recommendation: The Chief Executive shall require the physical count of the properties of the Municipal. A committee composed of three (3) members should be created for this purpose and the corresponding report on the physical Count of Property, Plant, and Equipment should be prepared following the instructions provided for in page 116 of Volume 2 of the Manual on the NGAS.
observations.managementAction: No action
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2016 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management discontinue the practice of appropriating wages under the Maintenance and Other Operating Services, except for actual job orders with specific project and duration. We further recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer charge wages to appropriate PS budget. And, the Municipal Accountant and Bookkeepers cautiously analyze payrolls and record wages using the appropriate PS expense account, in order to present fairly the account balances of the municipality.
observations.reason: Wages of job orders personnel are charged to different programs or projects due to PS Limitation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Social Worker Officer submit the required monitoring reports on time and assure that every liquidation report has complete documentation such as reimbursement expense receipts which should tally with the total expenses incurred per barangay.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive facilitate the immediate implementation and full completion of the projects/programs funded by the National Government Agencies in order not to delay further the benefits expected from the implementation of those PPAs especially if the funds were already received.  Pertaining to the unreleased funds for the projects, boost representation and coordination with the source agencies through the LGUs focal person to expedite release of funds if necessary, subject to the implementing guidelines of the projects to be undertaken.

We recommended that the Municipal-GSO together with the designated canvassers comply strictly with the required documentation of any procurement made by the municipality and comply with the  requirements provided under Sec. 4(6) of PD 1445  in making and/or securing quotations from private suppliers.
Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant check against the authenticity and completeness of canvass papers supporting shopping procurements, identify  the names and identities of persons  appearing as signatories in the quotations in behalf of the supplier transacting with the LGU and ensure that the basic supporting documents as mentioned are properly accomplished, taking into consideration the deficiencies noted, before certifying and processing the claims against the funds of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a Journal Entry Voucher as basis for recording in the General Journal, Journal of Checks Issued and Cash Disbursement Journal.  All disbursement vouchers together with their supporting documents must be accompanied with proper Journal Entry Vouchers which will be the basis of the Municipal Accountant record, classify and summarize all the LGUs transactions as required under Sections 5 of Volume I of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I, Section 2 and Section 31 of the same Manual, Volume II.

We recommend that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to inform
the payor that the checks were already stale in accordance with  Section  58
of NGAS Manual for LGU so that a new check can  be  replaced.  In  addition,
she has to submit explanation/justification for failure to take  appropriate
action on the matter considering the lapse of four (4) years, more  or  less
after the date of issuance of the subject checks.

We recommend that  Management  require  the  Municipal  Accountant  to
revert the stale  checks  to  the  Cash  accounts  and  to  the  appropriate
liability account for fair presentation of the  accounts  in  the  financial
statements. In addition, proper control and monitoring are to be adopted  by
the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all checks  issued  are  released  to
the respective payees much more that the checks involved are for  the  share
of the barangay from RPT collections and the mandatory aid to barangays.

We recommend  that  Management  imposed  stricter  sanctions  such  as
withholding of salaries or any amount due them in case of failure to  comply
pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002  and  Section  89  and
128 of PD N. 1445.

      Further, to strictly enforced the settlement of previously drawn  cash
advances.  The  prescribed  period  of  settlement  as  provided  for  under
existing rules and regulation must be strictly observed, otherwise,  no  new
cash advance shall be granted to those who fail to comply.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balance of
reciprocal accounts to reflect correct  balances  of  the  accounts  in  the
financial statements.

We recommend that  Management  ensure  that  the  Inventory  Committee
undertake  an  actual  count  of  physical  inventory  taking  of  all   the
municipality’s  PPE  to  determine  their  physical   existence,   location,
condition and other relevant information. Likewise, exert  extra  effort  to
analyze, review and work back on the available documents/records of the  PPE
to determine the details/breakdown and to  effect  adjustments  accordingly,
to reflect the correct balances of the accounts and  to  reconcile  the  GSO
and Accounting records.

We recommend that Management to require the  Municipal  Accountant  to
record the receipt of the motor vehicle and heavy equipment in the books  of
account for fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommend that the management to assess the  present  condition  of
all its existing public infrastructures  assets  and  recognize  it  in  the
financial statement in accordance with PPSAS 17. Also,  the  Office  of  the
Municipal Accountant to maintain  a  separate  subsidiary  ledger  for  each
type/class of infrastructure assets.

We recommend that the management to strictly follow the guidelines  on
the Local Roads Asset Management System provided in  the  COA  Circular  No.
2015-008 dated November 25, 2015 in accounting and reporting all  its  local
roads and road network system.

We recommend that Management to require the  Municipal  Accountant  to
strictly comply with the regulation on the  proper  booking-up  of  accounts
payable pursuant to Section 4(s) of NGAS in  order  to  fairly  present  the
account in the financial statement at year end. Also,  to  ensure  that  the
payable account to be  covered  with  duly  approved  disbursement  vouchers
supported with the required documents  such  as  Inspection  and  Acceptance
Report and bills as basis for recording the payables in the books.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to  direct  the  Municipal
Accountant to strictly observe the rules and regulations on the  preparation
of  the  financial  statements.  Also,  the  Municipal  Accountant  and  the
Municipal Budget Officer to reconcile monthly their  respective  records  to
avoid the accumulation of  the  reconciling  items  and  to  facilitate  the
reconciliation of their balances.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant  to  strictly  observe  the
guidelines on the preparation and presentation of the  Financial  Statements
specifically on the submission of a complete sets of financial statements.

We recommend that Management to  require  the  concerned  officers  to
strictly comply with  the  limit  for  appropriation  and  expenses  for  PS
representing 45% of the total annual income  from  regular  sources  by  the
municipality in the next preceding year.

We recommend that Management to provide justification on the hiring of
the 226 job order personnel, ensure that hiring of job  personnel  is  based
on necessity and urgency, and limit the hiring of  job  order  personnel  to
complement only the office where transactions are voluminous.

We reiterated our  prior  year’s  recommendation  that  Management  to
meticulously  and  judiciously  plan  the  procurement   process   and   the
procurement of goods to be made  through  competitive  bidding  pursuant  to
Sections 7 and 10 of the revised IRR of RA No.  9184  and  to  refrain  from
paying goods  on  reimbursement  basis  without  following  the  procurement
process provided in RA 9184 and its IRR.

We recommend that the management to strictly  observe  the  guidelines
on  the  implementation  of   infrastructure   project   by   administration
especially on hiring of manual labor through pakyaw contracting system.

We  recommend  that  management  to  submit  the  required  supporting
documentary requirements and the liquidation documents to  the  audit  team.
Also,  to  strictly  observe  the  rules  and  regulations   governing   the
utilization and reporting of the LDRRM Funds.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant  to  prepare  the  required
monthly report on the sources and utilization of LDRRMF and submit the  same
to the audit team within the prescribed period. Also, the LDRRMO to  furnish
the copy of the said report to the Office of the  Civil  Defense  and  DILG-
Local Government Operation Office.

We recommend that the  Local  Chief  Executive  direct  the  Municipal
Assessor to prioritize the titling of the municipal lots for its  protection
from third party claims.

We recommend that the designated GSO  to  account  the  handset  units
included in all the plan subscriptions paid/reimbursed by  the  LGU  and  to
issue the corresponding PAR or ARE to  the  accountable  officer  concerned.
Also, the accounting office  to  prepare  the  necessary  journal  entry  to
record the asset account and  the  possible  adjustment  to  other  affected
accounts.

We reiterate our previous recommendation that the Municipal  Treasurer
to return to the source agencies any balance of the fund  received  for  all
completed  projects.  Also,  for  the  Municipal  Accountant  to  prepare  a
subsidiary  ledger  for  individual  source  agency  indicating  the  amount
transferred and disbursed for easy reference and to immediately  submit  the
schedule to the Audit Team for verification.

We recommend for management to stop the  practice  of  charging  prior
years’  expenses  against  the  current  year’s  appropriation.  Also,   the
Municipal Budget Office to ensure that all valid money claims for  the  year
to be properly obligated and recorded as accounts payable  in  the  book  of
accounts of the LGU at the end of the year to avoid the  charging  of  prior
year’s obligations to the current  year’s  appropriation.  Furthermore,  the
accounting office to  prepare  the  necessary  adjusting  journal  entry  to
correct the overstatement of the recorded expense account.

We recommend that the management to revisit the policy on the grant of
mobile  load  allowance  considering  its  economy  and  efficient  use   of
resources in the performance of services and to stop granting internet  load
allowance to its officials.

We reiterate  our  recommendation  that  Management  ensure  that  the
required notification to the public and other form  of  announcements  shall
be made for PPAs including the requirements in  accordance  with  the  above
stated circular to further enhance transparency in government.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to send
written notices to the payees concerned on the existence of the stale checks, further
practice notifying payees one (1) month before a check becomes stale.

We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting
journal entries to restore the liabilities of the LGU by debiting Cash in Bank and
crediting the Accounts Payable account.

We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to
ascertain whether or not these payable accounts under the Trust Fund are still due
for remittance to concerned offices. If these are still due for remittance, instruct the
Municipal Treasurer to remit immediately the amount involved in order to protect
the interest of the employee-members concerned and clear the books of these prior
years’ payable accounts.

We recommend that management direct the Municipal Budget Officer and
the Municipal Accountant-Designate to charge and record the wages of the
casuals/job order employees to its appropriate account – Salaries and Wages-
Casual/Contractual as prescribed in the Philippines Public Sector Accounting
Standards (PPSAS).

We also recommend that management minimize the hiring of casuals/job
order employees and maximize the services of the permanent employees. Moreover,
prioritize the regularization of the casual and job order workers, who are
performing regular agency functions and specific vital activities or services, and are
eligible for permanent civil service.

We recommend that management submit justification on the deferment of
loan amortization and the incurrence of unnecessary interest expense of P169,797.90
and P2,071,534.38 in CY 2015 and 2016, respectively.

We also recommend that management discuss with the Development Bank of
the Philippines (DBP) on the possibility of reducing the payment terms by forgoing
the remaining grace period and immediately start paying the amortization of the
principal in order to save the remaining P2,920,523.88 in interest and charges.

We recommend that management adopt a modified salary schedule with
lower rates but at uniform percentage of the authorized rates for the First Tranche
Salary Increase pursuant to Section 4.3 of Local Budget Circular No. 108.

We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant re-compute and require
refund from the concerned employees the excess of the allowed salary increase.
Otherwise, disbursement vouchers and payrolls on the differential and salary
increase will be suspended/disallowed in audit.

We recommend that management submit the list prepared during the budget
preparation for CY 2016, enumerating the specific projects under the 20%
Development Fund. Henceforth, require from the Local Development Council a list
of duly identified projects in the formulation of future annual budgets.

We recommend that henceforth, management prohibits the extension of
contract time on project implementation due to (a) ordinary unfavorable weather
conditions and (b) inexcusable failure or negligence of contractor to provide the
required equipment, supplies or materials pursuant to Item 10.2, Annex E of the
Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184. Further, immediately
issue resume orders to fast track project implementation.

We recommend that management require the contractor to cause the
extension on the validity of the performance security once it expires while the
project is ongoing. Further, ensure that performance securities on all on-going
projects are still valid up to completion date, taking into consideration the time
extensions.

We recommend that management and the Vice Mayors’ League of the
Philippines (VMLP) – Bohol Chapter submit legal basis on the financial aid on top
of the term-membership fee and to submit justification on the relevance of the
activities in the Activity Design in the discharge of its primary function concerning
local legislation, otherwise, the financial aid will be disallowed/discontinued.

We recommend that management require the VMLP and PCL to submit the
basis in using multiple sets of receipts and the issuance of new sets of receipts
contrary to the agreement made during the December 17, 2014 COA and ULAP
meeting.

Further, advise the Municipal Treasurer to not accept the old receipts of the
leagues and allow only the use of official receipts procured from the National
Printing Office before the release of the membership fees, annual dues and
registration fees.

We recommend that management adhere to Sections 6.1.5 of COA-DBM-
DILG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 by limiting the release of cash advance for
Confidential Fund per quarter or the requirement for three (3) months only.

We also recommend that the Local Peace and Order Council determine the
proper release and utilization of the total CF by issuing a resolution duly approved
by two-thirds (2/3) of the total membership.

We recommend that management-
1. Instruct the LDRRMO to furnish a copy of the LDRRMF Investment Plan to
the OIC Municipal Accountant for the recording of the unexpended CYs
2011-2015 Quick Response Fund and DRRMF-MOOE in the Trust Fund as
Trust Liability, and to the Budget Officer for the recording of the DRRMF-
Capital Outlay as continuing appropriation in the SAAOB.
2. Require the OIC Municipal Treasurer to issue check to actually set aside and
transfer to the Trust Fund the unexpended LDRRMF (QRF & MOOE) for
CY 2011-2015.
3. Advise the Sanggunian to revert back to the General Fund the unexpended
balance of the CY 2010 LDRRMF and identify projects/programs for the
available funds.

We recommend that management require the LDRRMO to submit the
Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan
(LDRRMFIP) at the start of the year and the monthly Report of Sources and
Utilization of DRRMF duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant on or
before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommend that Management require all officials and employees
concerned to submit immediately the liquidation reports of their outstanding cash
advances and/or refund the unexpended cash balance, if any. Otherwise, direct the
Municipal Accountant to immediately withhold the payment of their salaries or any
amount due them.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to
transfer any amount in excess of the allowable maintaining balance to other existing
bank accounts with authorized government financial institutions (GFIs).

We further recommend that management request approval from the Bureau
of Local Government Finance (BLGF) to maintain accounts with banks not
compliant with the requirements by following Section 5.5 of the DOF Department
Circular 001-2015.

We recommend that management submit justification on the late submission
of perfected contracts and further require the BAC Chairman and the Secretariat to
furnish copies of the perfected contracts/purchase orders with complete supporting
documents within five (5) days after approval. Any unjustified failure of the
concerned officials and employees to comply shall be subjected to the administrative
disciplinary action under the penalty clause in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated
February 12, 2009.

We recommend that Management require the Municipal Accountant to
submit monthly trial balances on the 20th day of the following month to facilitate the
verification of transactions and reports of the LGU, as well as submit quarterly trial
balance and year-end reports on time.

We recommended that the Social Worker Officer submit the required monitoring reports on time and assure that every liquidation report has complete documentation such as total reimbursement expense receipt which should tally with the total expenses incurred per barangay.
observations.managementAction: Compliance reports were already submitted to COA Office dated March 15, 2016 by the MSWDO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MDRRM Officer and the MDRRM Council prepare and align their activities that could address the four (4) thematic areas completely in line with the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1
observations.managementAction: The Annual Investment Program (AIP) for Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF) for CY2016 were already divided into four (4) thematic areas in compliance with the audit recommendations. Compliance reports were already submitted to COA Office on March 31, 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management strictly adhere to the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 especially Section C.1.4 on the priority programs, projects and activities for GAD.

We further recommended that the Chairperson, GFPS-TWG identify clearly the GAD-related projects and monitor its compliance and implementation including the GAD Accomplishment Report on the other basic services of the Municipality which at the onset are not gender-responsive.  

Moreover, formulate a GAD Plan that would reflect the actual gender issues and concerns prevailing in the locality.
observations.managementAction: Compliance reports were already submitted to COA Office on March 31, 2016 by the MSWDO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local School Board prepare a more representative and effective annual budget for the Special Education Fund (SEF) in order to utilize available funds in productive activities and projects to achieve higher standards of education and better schools for the youth conducive to social development and advancement.  Effective budgeting and fast and timely implementation of programs/projects are necessary to maximize the use of the available funds.
observations.managementAction: The Local School Board prepared and approved the Supplemental Budget for CY2016 for the implementation of priority education projects in the municipality under SB Resolution No. 004 dated May 19, 2016 for the available funds under the SEF, in compliance with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management appropriate the declared idle general fund of the municipality amounting P11,000,000.00 that was invested in time deposit to development programs and projects in order that the needed services to the constituents shall not be delayed or sacrificed. Fast track implementation of the programmed PPAs.

Further, we recommended and management agreed to transfer back the Php8,000,000.00 time deposit plus interest to the Municipality’s current account maintained for its trust fund and hasten the implementation of programs/projects for which these trust funds were created.
observations.managementAction: Management have already requested the SB members for a resolution to terminate the time deposits under the General Fund and Trust Fund amounting to Php11,000,000.00 and Php8,000,000.00, respectively, to transfer back the time deposits to the current account of the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the incumbent Local Chief Executive (LCE) refer the matter to the Civil Service Commission (CSC) being the proper agency to decide on matters involving payment of terminal leave benefits of government officials and employees. In case the CSC decided in favor of the claimant, the municipality shall require submission of the complete documentary requirements as enumerated under Section 5.13 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, before payment of terminal leave benefits shall be made.
observations.managementAction: The former municipal official, being the recipient of the said cash advance and determined as person liable, executed a waver allowing management to deduct directly the unliquidated cash advance from her claim for terminal leave benefits. Hence, the amount of Php400,000.00 was already refunded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to prepare and submit a Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) as of December 31, 2015 not later than January 31 to the Office of the Auditor as well as the Office of the Municipal Accountant to aid the latter reconcile her records based on the existence and completeness of the PPE and to assist the Auditor in the verification on the Property, Plant and Equipment of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) was already submitted by the General Services Office (GSO) to the COA Office on April 1, 2016 and to the Accounting Office   for reconciliation in the books of accounts of the municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to complete the documents needed to finally drop the unserviceable properties and obsolete items from the books following the process prescribed by the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management in Local Government as prescribed by COA Circular No. 92-386.  Further, management agreed to follow strictly the prescribed format of the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties by the NGAs Manual for Local Government Units to provide a better and accurate report.
observations.managementAction: The GSO have prepared the updated lists of unserviceable properties of the municipality and likewise conducted physical count of the unserviceable properties. The request for COA appraisal and disposal have already been acted upon by COA Technical Audit Specialist. The municipality is still in the process of disposing  the obsolete and unserviceable properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to submit a copy of the contracts and purchase orders within five days from issuance as required under COA Circular 2009-001, together with the bidding documents, BAC resolutions, minutes of the proceedings, abstract, and such other documents peculiar to the contract and/or to the mode of procurement which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.
observations.managementAction: Management complied faithfully to the furnishing of copies of purchase orders and its supporting documents   to the COA Office. However, copies of infrastructure contracts were not furnished, contrary to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management require the Accountant to prepare the BRS to be able to identify reconciling items and possible erroneous recordings and mis-postings of transactions in the books and in the bank.

Likewise, we recommend that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to give priority in updating her cash books and reconcile the same with the Accounting unit monthly.
observations.managementAction: Eight BRS were prepared.
observations.reason: BRS not prepared for all the Bank accounts of the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management initiate measures to collect the delinquent accounts from market rentals and waterworks system amounting to P668, 270.00.

The Sanggunian Bayan should enact a new tax ordinance on market and waterworks operations and to include the imposition of penalties and surcharges for non or late payment to ensure them to comply with their obligations and avoid the incurrence of delinquencies.
observations.managementAction: Issued demand letter
observations.reason: The balance decreased to P469,950.00 and 89,498.50, respectively during CY 2016 because of the settlement made after the demand letter has been issued. However, most of the balance prior to CY 2009 were still uncollected.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Management require the Disbursing Officer to give priority in the posting of transactions in her cash books. Failure to liquidate cash advances at year end tends to understate expenses by an amount equivalent to the portion of such cash advances already expended as at the same date.
observations.managementAction: Liquidation reports were submitted and posted in the cashbook

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should institute action for the collection of the accounts on the known beneficiaries by sending them demand letters. If warranted, request for the write-off of the accounts as provided for in COA Circular No. 97-001.
observations.managementAction: Assessment was made by the MSWDO and found-out that some of the creditors were already dead and have transferred. Those creditors that were found during the assessment promised to pay.
observations.reason: The management did not send demand letter to the creditors and no request for write-off was made for those dead AOs.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management should require the Property Officer or the General Service Officer to prepare the report of properties that require insurance coverage and submit the same to the GSIS to ensure that government properties will be protected against all insurable risks.
observations.managementAction: Public market was insured. Other buildings for insurance have been inspected by the GSIS for determination of the premiums.
observations.reason: The insurance of other buildings were still in process.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Accountant should ensure that entries made for the transactions are checked and verified to avoid improper classification of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Proper classification of accounts was observed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer return to the source agencies any balance of the fund received for all completed projects. 

We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare a subsidiary ledger for individual source agency indicating the amount transferred and disbursed for easy reference.
observations.managementAction: Some balances of funds received from national government agencies were refunded after the completion of the project.
observations.reason:
There are still huge amount not yet returned to the source agencies.

Reiterated in 2016 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to observe strictly the provisions cited under Chapter 5 (B) of the NGAS Manual, Volume I and to draw Journal Entry Voucher to close the remaining balance of the Government Equity under the Trust Fund to Government Equity under the General Fund.
observations.managementAction: The account has been transferred to the general fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant withhold the corresponding taxes due on the honoraria being given to members of the BAC in compliance with Section 2.79 of the BIR Regulation No. 10-2008 dated July 8, 2008.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant has complied with the recommendation

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management should designate a mechanic or a person knowledgeable in motor vehicles to conduct pre and post-inspection activities.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant should look into the attachment of complete supporting documents before the approval of claims.

Likewise, the stop the payment of procured items thru reimbursements and pre-procurement documents should be properly filled up including the official receipts.
observations.managementAction: Hired mechanical engineer to conduct pre and post-inspection of motor vehicles to be repaired.
observations.reason: Some claims still lacked the necessary supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal School Board should include only items which are within the power and authority granted to the Local School Board to maximize the use of the Special Education Fund for the benefit of its intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal School Board complied for CY 2016 SEF budget

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant instruct her staff that in the preparation of payroll for subsistence and laundry allowances of the personnel in the RHU to consider the provisions as provided in No. 8.2 and 10.2 of DBM-DOH Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2012 to avoid refund.
observations.managementAction: Subsistence allowance of MHO employees was deducted for every day of absence.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend management:

1.	To require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that claims for infrastructure projects have to be supported with documents enumerated in Section 9 of COA Circular 2012-001 and Annex A of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

2.	To require the Municipal Engineer/ BAC Secretariat to indicate all the necessary data in the contract and bidding documents to easily determine the compliance by the contractor of its responsibilities on the contract.

3.	That effective immediately, all construction, rehabilitation and concreting of municipal properties/facilities have to supported with estimate of work, drawing/plans/specification and program of work to prevent wastage of materials and losses of government resources.
observations.managementAction: Contracts and its supporting documents were submitted only upon demand.
observations.reason: Some of the projects of the LGU were suspended in audit due to lack of supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that in the ensuing years, the Local Chief Executive should instruct the Municipal Budget Office to immediately submit to the SP the action taken on the findings and recommendations observed by the LFC so that the appropriation ordinance shall be declared operative in its entirety.
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive through the Municipal Budget Officer acted immediately on the recommendation made by the reviewing authority.          The SB also acted through the passing of Resolution No. 0314 dated July 14, 2015.       However, it was submitted by the SB Secretary to the Sangguniang Panlalawigan late.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the BAC of the Municipality implement the standard rate on the sale of bidding documents as prescribed under GPPB Resolution No 04-2012.
observations.managementAction: Management implemented the standard rate prescribed under the GPPB resolution.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to instruct the General Service Officer and Municipal Engineer, ensure the timely submission of copies of government contracts and purchase orders including Inspection and Acceptance Reports as provided for in COA Circulars No. 2009-001 and 95-006.
observations.managementAction: Submitted purchase orders
observations.reason: Contracts and its supporting documents were submitted only upon demand.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Bids and Award Committee (BAC) to submit promptly all contracts with their supporting documents to the Audit Team for review within five (5) days from perfection thereof in compliance with existing regulations.
observations.managementAction: Contracts were submitted upon demand except for the water system which is implemented by administration

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor allocate funds from their budget for the registration of all parcels of land in the name of the municipality for its protection from third party.
observations.managementAction: Appropriation for titling of properties was already incorporated in the Budget.
observations.reason:
Application for titling is still in process.

Reiterated in 2016 AAR

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the OIC-Municipal Accountant should strictly adhere to the accounting guidelines and procedures provided in the New Government Accounting System Manual in recording all transactions of the municipal government.
Likewise, she should maintain the General Journals, General Ledgers and Subsidiary Ledgers.  All transactions shall be recorded in the General Journal through the Journal Entry Voucher.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant’s Office is already maintaining journal entry vouchers and a subsidiary ledgers
observations.reason: No general journal and general ledger were maintained.

observations.recommendation: Require the Accountant to submit the Monthly and Quarterly Trial Balances and Disbursement Vouchers with the supporting documents within the prescribed period as required by existing regulations to avoid sanctions for non-compliance of the requirement and the possible institution of disciplinary action against the erring employee.
observations.managementAction: The accounting office submitted the Financial reports and the DVs.
observations.reason: Financial reports and the DVs were submitted after the prescribed period and sometimes upon demand.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that all collections should be deposited intact daily to the Liquidating Officer and/or authorized depository bank strictly in accordance with the provision cited under Section 32 of COA Circular 2002-003 and Joint Circular 1-81 of DOF and COA.
observations.managementAction: The liquidating Officer was instructed to advise all collectors to turn overall collections daily as required by existing regulations.
observations.reason: Some collectors do not remit collections daily.

observations.recommendation: The Bids Awards Committee or the head of the procuring entity should  secure insurance and provide funds to pay for indem-nification benefits of the BAC Members and its supports staff as provided for under Section 73 RA 9184 and GPPB Resolution 21-2005
observations.managementAction: Allocated funds for the purpose.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries to transfer the cost of completed project to the appropriate asset account in order to present fairly the account balances of the municipality, pursuant to Section 50 of NGAS, Volume 1.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entry was made at the beginning of the year to transfer the cost of the New Public Market to the appropriate asset account.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to ascertain whether or not these payable accounts under the Trust Fund are still due for remittance to concerned offices. If these are still due for remittance, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to remit immediately the amount involved in order to protect the interest of the employee-members concerned and clear the books of these prior years’ payable accounts.
observations.managementAction: Verification has been made on the accounts. The prior year’s totaling P171,096.52 represent the amount after reconciliation/settlements.
observations.reason: Old accounts still to be traced.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that succeeding cash advances be supported with a comprehensive Physical and Financial Plan in order to establish basis on the disbursement of the fund.
observations.managementAction: Definite program, activity or project has been indicated to support the disbursement of CF.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant or his authorized representative to submit all succeeding cash advance vouchers for confidential program/activities within seven (7) working days from release of the check.
observations.managementAction: In 2016, the CA voucher for CF and the supporting documents were submitted within the prescribed time.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer any amount in excess of the allowable maintaining balance to other existing bank accounts with authorized government financial institutions (GFIs). We further recommend that management request approval from the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF) to maintain accounts with banks not compliant with the requirements under DOF Circular 001-2015.
observations.managementAction: Maintained the balance of not more than P500,000.00.
observations.reason: Request for approval to maintain the account with non-GFI is still on process. (Reiterated in Part II Finding No. 14)

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the BAC Chairman and Secretariat to submit copies of the perfected contracts/purchase orders, together with the complete supporting documents within five (5) days after approval in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: Contracts perfected in CY 2015 were submitted.
observations.reason: BAC Chairman and Secretariat have been busy with other functions that is why contracts in CY 2016 were not submitted on time. (Reiterated in Part II Finding No. 15)

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant adhere with the prescribed period in the submission of accounts and financial reports. Failure to submit on time shall automatically cause the suspension of payment of their salaries pursuant to Sections 122 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: Deficient reports for CY 2015 were submitted.
observations.reason: In CY 2016, reports were not submitted on time due to increased volume of transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management ensure that only programs and activities that address gender-related issues are included in the GAD Plan and Budget. Further, require the Focal Person to submit the GAD Plan and Budget to the Philippine Commission on Women (PCW) and a received file be submitted to the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Plan and Budget was submitted to MLGOO for the DILG’s issuance of certificate as to compliance. Further, require the Focal Person to submit the GAD Plan and Budget to the Philippine Commission on Women (PCW) and a received file be submitted to the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: Require the inventory committee to conduct a complete physical count of all properties and submit the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) and Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Auditor in accordance with Section 114 and 115 of NGAS Manual for LGUs; Likewise, reconciliation between the records maintained by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant should be made and effect appropriate adjustments when necessary.
observations.managementAction: The report of the physical count in CY 2014 was submitted to the auditor on April 17, 2016. The report of the physical count in CY 2015 was also not submitted on time.
observations.reason: The report of the physical count in CY 2016 was not yet submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the local government unit return the PDAF balance to the Bureau of Treasury for reversion to the unappropriated surplus in the general fund of the national government in accordance with the Supreme Court decision dated November 19, 2013.
observations.managementAction: The amount of P202,847.00 was utilized, while the amount of P489.00 was returned to DSWD.
observations.reason: The remaining P64,097.81 are old accounts, still subject for verification where to return the balance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the OIC-Municipal Accountant prepare the report and submit as soon as possible its Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation for the AAR 2013. It is further recommended that management comply with the provision and submit the action plan within sixty (60) days upon receipt of the AAR 2014 and its ensuing years.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared the AAPSI. However, submission was delayed.
observations.reason: The assigned personnel have been busy with other works/function.

observations.managementAction: The accounting section had maintained the updated SLC records. No reconciliation.
observations.reason: No assigned personnel to do the reconciliation between the Property Cards and the Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment of the Municipal Treasurer’s Office with the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) of the Accounting Unit.

observations.managementAction: Monthly Reports of Official Travels and Report of Fuel Consumption were prepared. However, the designated employee may not be able to furnish the Auditor with the copies of the reports.
observations.reason: Instead of consolidating the official travels and the fuel consumptions of all government vehicles in one month, reports were made per vehicle which were attached to the DVs.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to management that effective immediately, all municipal officials and employees paying dues and attending seminar/conferences conducted by leagues/associations with registration fees should demand government official receipts to acknowledge the payment for proper accounting of collections.
observations.managementAction: Municipal officials and employees were informed about the issue. However, they opined that they don’t have any control as to what receipt will be issued by the leagues or associations. During the Conference of COA Officials with the Union of Local Authorities of the Philippines, Inc. (ULAP), it was agreed that receipts currently used by leagues are still acceptable until all are utilized.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management:
1. Require the municipal treasurer at the beginning of the year to prepare the certified list of taxpayer with the amount due and collectible for the year and submit to the municipal accountant for recording.
2. Require the municipal treasurer to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the debit to Real Property Tax Receivable/Special Education Tax Receivable and the credit to Deferred Real Property Tax Income/Deferred Special Education Tax Income based on the list submitted by the municipal treasurer.
3. Require the municipal treasurer to prepare a current list of delinquent taxpayers with the amount due and collectible and submit to the municipal accountant for reconciliation of records to establish a correct balance of RPT/SET Receivables.
4. Require the municipal accountant to make the necessary adjustment in the books based on the correct RPT/SET balance.
observations.managementAction: A certified list of taxpayers had been submitted for recording. Based on the certified list of taxpayers a Journal Entry Voucher was drawn to set up the RPT/SET Receivables for CY 2014. The Municipal Treasurer had submitted a current list of delinquent taxpayers which was used as basis to adjust the RPT/SET receivable balance.
observations.reason: Reconciliation of records is still on process due to a sizeable number of taxpayers.

observations.managementAction: Liquidations of some funds were submitted. For reclassification in the coming year. Review of records was made but refund or request for authority to use the fund was not done. Confirmation to the issuing agency on the fund status was not made.
observations.reason: Some of the projects were still going on.

observations.managementAction: No disposal was made.
observations.reason: Records for disposal not yet completed.

observations.managementAction: The accounting section maintained the updated SLC records. No reconciliation.
observations.reason: No reconciliation between the Property Cards and the Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment of the Municipal Treasurer’s Office with the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) of the Accounting Unit.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to ensure prompt and full implementation of the 20% developmental projects programmed for the current year in order to achieve the intended purposes for which the fund was appropriated.
observations.managementAction: CY 2012 projects were already implemented.
observations.reason: Current year’s 20% Development Fund were not fully implemented since the LGU is also the implementing agency of several projects of the national government.
